# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for quick setup.

Parameters:
  LatestAmiId:
    Type: 'AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<AWS::EC2::Image::Id>'
    Default: '/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/amzn2-ami-hvm-x86_64-gp2'
    Description: The latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI ID.
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t2.micro
    Description: The EC2 instance type.
  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instances.
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: The VPC ID where the EC2 instance will be launched.
  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: The Subnet ID where the EC2 instance will be launched.
  AppEBSVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
    Description: Size of the application data EBS volume in GB.

Resources:
  SSHSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Enable SSH access via port 22
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-SSHSecurityGroup"

  AppInstance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !Ref LatestAmiId
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          DeviceIndex: '0'
          GroupSet:
            - !GetAtt SSHSecurityGroup.GroupId
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        - DeviceName: /dev/sdh # Common device name for additional EBS volumes on Linux
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref AppEBSVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp2
            DeleteOnTermination: true # For Phase 1, volume deletes with instance
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-AppInstance"
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Development # Example tag

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: The ID of the EC2 instance.
    Value: !Ref AppInstance
  PublicIp:
    Description: The Public IP address of the EC2 instance.
    Value: !GetAtt AppInstance.PublicIp
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: The ID of the Security Group created for the EC2 instance.
    Value: !GetAtt SSHSecurityGroup.GroupId
